news.headline: With World Distracted by Iran War, Israeli Settlers Intensify Attacks in West Bank
news.summary: Israel has failed to arrest a wave of violence against Palestinians, prompting military officials to urge the government to intervene.

news.headline: Trump says US operation will aid ships stranded in Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: President Donald Trump said on Sunday the U.S. would start an effort on Monday morning to free ships stranded in the Strait ​of Hormuz as a "humanitarian gesture" to aid neutral countries in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran. news.headline: Europe, Canada leaders hold Yerevan talks in Trump's shadow
news.summary: European leaders and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney gather in Armenia Monday as they seek to navigate a fraught geopolitical environment under an unpredictable White House. news.headline: Australian inquiry opens public hearings into Bondi Beach shooting
news.summary: An inquiry into a shooting that killed 15 people at a Hanukkah festival near Australia's Bondi Beach heard Monday that Sydney's Jewish community feared "catastrophe" was coming as antisemitic attacks rose. news.headline: Asia View: Taiwan’s President reaches Eswatini after China-linked travel hurdles
news.summary: In our Asia View segment, FRANCE 24 correspondent Yena Lee takes a look at the latest stories behind the headlines from China and beyond. news.headline: Chasing Stardom in Korea Nearly Destroyed Ejae. Then Came ‘KPop Demon Hunters.’
news.summary: Ejae, the movie’s breakout star, was the face of K-pop in 2025. Facing questions of art, national belonging and authenticity, she wants to be much more.

news.headline: China says to ignore US sanctions even as Trump visit nears
news.summary:
China ordered companies to ignore US sanctions against five oil refiners accused of buying Iranian crude, an unprecedented pushback against American financial measures less than two weeks before a presidential summit.

news.headline: Inside The Old Skydiving Plane Hunting Drones in Ukraine
news.summary: As Russian drones overwhelm Ukraine’s vast air defenses, civilian volunteers are stepping in to take them down. The New York Times takes flight with a crew that’s turned an old skydiving plane into a drone hunter.

news.headline: Can Malaysia’s Umno reclaim role as pillar of Malay political power with solo election bid?
news.summary:
Malaysia’s Umno is making a heady gamble by planning to strike out on its own, experts say, as the former ruling party looks to rally support from among the country’s Malay-Muslim majority in preparation for national polls that must be called in less than two years from now.

news.headline: Should kitesurf be banned? French mayor wants to ban sport to keep sea swimmers safe
news.summary: Should kitesurfing be banned for the sake of keeping sea swimmers safe? That is certainly what one mayor in France wants. In Saint-Pierre-Quiberon, several accidents have already occurred, but the question of whether or not the water sport should be banned is making ripples.
